description:
Trident evolutionary high-leverage move (Claude framing, supported by Codex's "chrome budget" and Gemini's "Pluggable Tool-Shelf"): the toolbar buttons (Terminal/Browser/Markdown/SplitH/SplitV/+) are c11 concerns living inside a generic split-tab library. Extracting them into a c11-side accessory slot does four things at once:

1. Eliminates the bug class structurally (host owns the chrome it provides; bonsplit just reserves space for whatever the host hands it).
2. Shrinks bonsplit toward genuine reusability for a second Stage 11 project.
3. Moves c11-specific concepts (terminal/browser/markdown surface kinds) to the host that owns them.
4. Spins a flywheel: Lattice or future Stage 11 projects could use bonsplit with a different accessory cluster.

Sketch: bonsplit exposes a `trailingAccessory` view-builder slot on `TabBarView`, with measured intrinsic width feeding back into `trailingTabContentInset`. Bonsplit no longer hard-codes button identities or counts; the constant goes away entirely.

Strategic, not tactical. File-and-let-mature; revisit if (a) third regression of this bug class occurs, or (b) we need bonsplit in a second project.

relationships_out.note: Strategic evolutionary direction surfaced during CMUX-22 review
Atin direction 2026-04-19: skip tactical fix, go direct to architectural answer
CMUX-31's measurement mechanism becomes CMUX-30 Phase 1 (internal plumbing + EmptyView default, before host migration)

title: Bonsplit ↔ c11: TrailingAccessory slot — extract toolbar buttons out of bonsplit
